metadata.skill_revisions: Intake now asks about jargon tolerance. Lesson body must lead with concept map BEFORE files. ASCII diagrams encouraged for multi-component flows. No preambles. No structural-tour lessons. Curriculum template: no dates, replaced 'Notes from sessions' with terse 'Mentor scratchpad'.

expectations.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions) BEFORE proposing the curriculum.
expectations.evidence: Intake recap shows goal sharpness + jargon tolerance + scope questions answered. No 'great topic' preamble.

expectations.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved (copied into outputs/curriculum.md).
expectations.evidence: curriculum.md exists, 28 lines, no dates.

expectations.text: Curriculum contains a clear single-paragraph Goal section.
expectations.evidence: Concrete: 'take a pjb-... ID from a pager alert and reason from first principles about WHERE in the pipeline it is stuck'.

expectations.text: Curriculum has between 3 and 7 lessons.
expectations.evidence: 6 lessons.

expectations.text: Curriculum or lesson 1 references at least 3 real file paths from this repo.
expectations.evidence: parsing_v2.py:751, service.py:1559, llamaparseMultiActivityWorkflow.ts:467, llamaparseWorkflow.ts:67 — all line-counts verified by agent.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 opens with an explicit, verifiable goal statement.
expectations.evidence: 'draw the box-and-arrow diagram from memory, name the FIVE places a job can wedge, and explain WHY the team split the work this way.'

expectations.text: Lesson 1 grounds in specific repo files first, then generalizes.
expectations.evidence: Now leads with the CONCEPT MAP (three actors, two reliability layers) + ASCII diagram BEFORE file refs — matches the iteration-2 ordering rule. Generalizes to SEDA / stage-and-queue pipelining.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active comprehension check.
expectations.evidence: Three scenarios: last event ActivityTaskScheduled vs ActivityTaskStarted vs explain-the-asymmetric-timeout. All active, all predictive.

expectations.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions) BEFORE producing the lesson list.
expectations.evidence: 3-axis intake. Jargon tolerance noted in scratchpad: 'first-occurrence gloss; don't over-define on second use.'

expectations.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved to outputs/curriculum.md.
expectations.evidence: curriculum.md exists.

expectations.text: Curriculum has 4-6 lessons (user asked for ~5).
expectations.evidence: 5 lessons.

expectations.text: Curriculum visibly covers BOTH repo-specific Temporal usage AND general workflow-engine concepts.
expectations.evidence: Each lesson 'closes with the general concept climb'. Lessons 1-4 anchor in real repo files; the bridge to general concepts is explicit.

expectations.text: Curriculum or lesson 1 references at least 2 real Temporal-related file paths.
expectations.evidence: extract/temporal/workflow.py, parse/temporal/direct/activity.py, plus pointers to error_handling.py, non_retryable.py, dynamic_worker.py, registry/.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 starts from real code in this repo and then ladders up to general Temporal concepts.
expectations.evidence: Lesson 1 explicitly structured: (a) concept map first WITH ASCII diagram → (b) ground in workflow.py + activity.py contrast → (c) climb to durable-execution / event-sourcing → (d) compare with table → (e) check. Matches the iteration-2 ordering rule cleanly.

expectations.text: Curriculum touches on Temporal's tradeoffs vs. alternatives.
expectations.evidence: Comparison table with RabbitMQ+status, AWS Step Functions, Airflow, Cadence. Honest answer for why this team picked Temporal.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active exercise.
expectations.evidence: Predict-then-read + 'spot the bug' (uuid.uuid4() inside workflow) with three sub-questions on when/why/fix.

expectations.text: Response includes an intake exchange (1-3 questions) before the curriculum.
expectations.evidence: 3-axis intake. Scratchpad shows calibration: 'user has shipped to but not operated the cluster — bias toward what would I see when this breaks.'

expectations.text: A curriculum markdown file is saved to outputs/curriculum.md.
expectations.evidence: curriculum.md exists.

expectations.text: Curriculum references at least 3 real file paths under infra/.
expectations.evidence: keda.tf, keda-scaledobject.yaml, llamacloud/templates/, terraform/environments/prod/main.tf, terraform/modules/eks/main.tf etc.

expectations.text: Curriculum has 3-7 lessons.
expectations.evidence: 6 lessons.

expectations.text: Curriculum covers general k8s/EKS concepts.
expectations.evidence: Control plane vs data plane, IRSA / workload identity, cluster autoscaling tradeoffs (Karpenter vs CA vs MNG), HPA vs KEDA push/pull metrics, GitOps.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 starts from a real file or set of files in infra/ and ladders to a general concept.
expectations.evidence: Lesson 1 is NO LONGER a directory tour. Now opens with the provisioning-vs-workload concept + ASCII diagram, then shows ONE file from each side (keda.tf vs keda-scaledobject.yaml) as a contrast pair, then generalizes to the two-tier infra pattern. Directly addresses the iteration-1 'too short and basic' feedback.

expectations.text: Lesson 1 ends with an active comprehension check.
expectations.evidence: Three-part check: sorting test (6 items, classify each side of the seam), failure-mode test ('I deleted keda.tf...'), seam-finding test (open the dir, list operator installs). All active, scenario-based, not trivia.

notes: Assertion pass rate is unchanged at 100% — the iteration-1 rubric was already saturated. The real iteration-2 signal is qualitative: do the lessons feel less basic, do diagrams help, did 'no dates' and 'no journaling' clean up the curriculum file?

Concrete improvements observed in iter-2 outputs: (a) ALL three lessons now open with a concept map paragraph + ASCII diagram BEFORE dropping into file references — the inversion the user explicitly asked for. (b) Curriculum files have zero datestamps; the 'Mentor scratchpad' section is terse and calibration-focused, not session-journal. (c) The k8s lesson 1 — the one the user called 'too short and basic' — has been completely reframed: it now lands the provisioning-vs-workload seam concept with a contrast pair (keda.tf vs keda-scaledobject.yaml) and ends on a three-scenario check including 'I deleted keda.tf, why is helm upgrade failing?'. No longer a directory tour.
Slightly faster + slightly fewer tokens than iter-1, suggesting the 'no preamble' guidance is reducing throat-clearing without hurting depth. The temporal run especially: 216s vs 332s in iter-1.

What the v1->v2 viewer comparison will show: same prompts, two versions of each output side by side. Differences are stylistic (ordering, diagrams, scratchpad cleanup), not structural — both versions ask intake, save curriculum, run lesson 1, comprehension check. The bet is the iteration-2 ordering reads better to the user than iteration-1.
